coaching.coaching: Pin down each acceptance criterion's observable outcome with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: each acceptance criterion's observable outcome

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: sufficient
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between the declared dependency on the criterion-integration child and the fully-hardcoded `project.portability` id: either assert that `project.portability` IS the final id (making the dependency informational) or replace the hardcoded id with a placeholder and gate implementation explicitly on the child's output.
evidence: "Depends on the criterion-integration child because the move trigger must use the final activated criterion id."
"applies_when: [project.portability]" (Approach)
"The loaded move declares only `project.portability` in `applies_when`" (AC: move_trigger)
"The move is applicable for the trigger set `{project.portability}`" (AC: applies_to_portability)
findings.finding: The Dependencies section declares a prerequisite on a 'criterion-integration child' to finalize the criterion id, yet the Approach, ACs, and JSON spec all hardcode `project.portability` throughout with no caveat or placeholder — an implementer cannot tell whether to wait for the child or proceed with the embedded id, and a child-driven id change would silently invalidate every AC and the JSON key.
findings.location: Dependencies section vs. Approach section and all Acceptance Criteria
scenarios: Implementer proceeds immediately using `project.portability`, child later activates a differently-named criterion id, all ACs and the JSON file silently fail
Implementer waits for child before writing any code, contradicting the plan's fully-specified ACs
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Dependencies section: 'The criterion id `project.portability` is already confirmed as the final activated id from the criterion-integration child; this plan may proceed in parallel.' If the id is not yet confirmed, replace all occurrences of `project.portability` with a placeholder (e.g., `<portability-criterion-id>`) and mark the ACs as pending child resolution.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief doc entry (e.g., a `## Project Move Overlay` section in CLAUDE.md or a short ADR) capturing the `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` contract, the `strict=False` merge behaviour, and how to add new project-scoped coaching moves.
evidence: Plan states `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` is 'In scope' (being created), yet 'documentation' is explicitly listed as Out of scope
No ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc update appears anywhere in the plan
The plan describes the overlay contract ('JSON object keyed by move id', merge with `strict=False`) inline in prose — this is an architectural decision with no persistent capture
Future contributors adding a second project move, or auditing why `project.portability` fires, have no canonical reference beyond the test files
findings.finding: The plan introduces `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` as a new project-level config extension point (and a new trigger contract `project.portability`), but no documentation artifact — ADR, CLAUDE.md entry, or design note — is planned to capture this overlay pattern for future contributors.
scenarios: A new contributor adds a second project criterion and doesn't know `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` is the extension point
Someone auditing criterion triggers cannot find the overlay contract documented anywhere outside test code and this ephemeral plan
findings.suggested_fix: Add a paragraph to `CLAUDE.md` (or create `.rebar/docs/adr-project-move-overlay.md`) describing: (1) `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` is the project extension point for coaching moves, (2) keys follow the same schema as built-in moves, (3) `applies_when` must reference an activated criterion id, (4) the file is merged over the built-in registry at load time with `strict=False`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction: either confirm `project.portability` is the final, locked criterion id from the criterion-integration child (cite the child ticket's AC), or make this plan explicitly provisional by replacing the hardcoded id in the Approach and ACs with a placeholder and noting it will be filled in post-dependency.
evidence: Plan Approach: 'applies_when: [project.portability]'
Plan Dependencies: 'Depends on the criterion-integration child because the move trigger must use the final activated criterion id.'
Repo-wide grep for 'project.portability' returns zero hits in src/ — the criterion does not yet exist in the codebase.

AC lines: 'The loaded move declares only project.portability in applies_when', 'The move is applicable for the trigger set {project.portability}', 'The move is absent for the unrelated trigger set {F1}' — all bake in the unconfirmed id.
findings.finding: The plan hardcodes `project.portability` as the criterion id throughout (Approach JSON spec, all 10 ACs, test function descriptions), yet the Dependencies section simultaneously declares the final id depends on a 'criterion-integration child'. No `project.portability` id exists anywhere in the live codebase. If the child delivers a different id, every committed artifact — the `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` `applies_when` value and all test assertions — is wrong and must be reworked. The plan presents the id as settled while admitting it is not.
findings.location: Approach (applies_when spec) / Dependencies section / All 10 Acceptance Criteria
scenarios: The criterion-integration child activates the id as 'portability' or 'P-port' rather than 'project.portability'; the `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` file and all test assertions fail silently until manually updated.
The criterion-integration child is delayed or redesigned; this plan ships with a trigger that never fires because no finding carries the hardcoded id.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the Approach: 'The id `project.portability` is the expected output of ticket [criterion-integration child id]; if that child changes the id, update `applies_when` in `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` and the corresponding test assertions accordingly.' Alternatively, verify the id is already locked in the child and cite the child's AC that confirms it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit contract between this ticket and the criterion-integration child that pins the final criterion id (e.g. a named constant or a cross-ticket AC) so that a rename breaks a test rather than silently disabling the coaching move.
evidence: Criterion id `project.portability` appears zero times in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` and zero times in `src/rebar/`.
Dependencies section: 'Depends on the criterion-integration child because the move trigger must use the final activated criterion id.'
Approach section hardcodes: 'applies_when: [project.portability]' and 'The move is applicable for the trigger set {project.portability}'.
AC `move_trigger`: 'The loaded move declares only `project.portability` in `applies_when`.' — but no such criterion exists yet.
findings.finding: The `applies_when` value `project.portability` used in the new move and hardcoded across all ACs is a forward-reference to a criterion id that does not yet exist in the codebase (`criteria_routing.json` has no such entry, and `src/rebar` has no occurrences). The plan's Dependencies section acknowledges a 'criterion-integration child' that must finalize this id first, yet the Approach section and every AC pin the literal string `project.portability`. If the criterion-integration child lands under a different id (or is renamed), every AC in this ticket would silently fail (the move trigger would never fire), with no test catching the mismatch.
findings.location: Approach section / AC lines `move_trigger`, `applies_to_portability`, `ignores_unrelated_findings` / Dependencies section
scenarios: Criterion-integration child ships with id `project.client-portability` or `portability` rather than `project.portability`; the move trigger never intersects any real finding; coaching is silently dead for all portability findings.
A reviewer runs the ACs against a pre-criterion-integration state of the repo and every portability applicability test fails because the trigger set never resolves.
findings.suggested_fix: In the criterion-integration child, export the activated criterion id as a named constant (e.g. `PROJECT_PORTABILITY_CRITERION_ID = "project.portability"`) and import that constant in both `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json`'s test and the new coaching test — so a rename propagates mechanically rather than via grep.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `project.portability` is already confirmed as the final activated criterion id from the criterion-integration child (making the dependency informational) or is still pending — if confirmed, say so explicitly in the Approach; if not, add a note that the JSON key and `applies_when` value must be updated once the child delivers its final id.
evidence: "Depends on the criterion-integration child because the move trigger must use the final activated criterion id." (Dependencies section)
"applies_when: [project.portability]" hardcoded in the Approach section JSON spec
All 10 ACs hardcode `project.portability` with no caveat or placeholder (e.g., AC move_trigger: 'The loaded move declares only `project.portability` in `applies_when`')
triggers = {c for f in coachable for c in f.get('criteria', []) or []} (workflow_ops.py:380) — the trigger set is derived from finding criteria[], not an arbitrary string
Pass-1 filter in passes.py:326 admits only criterion IDs that are in the chunk's rubric, so the trigger can only appear if the criterion is activated
findings.finding: The plan has a hidden ordering/atomicity assumption: all ACs and the JSON spec fully hardcode `project.portability` as the criterion id, but the Dependencies section states 'the move trigger must use the final activated criterion id' (implying it is not yet confirmed). The trigger mechanism only fires if a surviving Pass-1 finding carries exactly `project.portability` in its criteria[] — which requires the criterion-integration child to have activated that precise id. If the child activates a different id, the JSON key, the `applies_when` value, and every AC silently become wrong, yet the plan gives no indication whether `project.portability` is already the confirmed final id.
findings.location: Dependencies section vs. Approach section and all Acceptance Criteria
scenarios: Criterion-integration child activates a different id (e.g., `project.portability.v2` or `G9`); the JSON file and all ACs are silently wrong; the move never fires in production.
Implementer proceeds with `project.portability` as-is but the dependency child has not yet run, producing a live mismatch between the JSON trigger and the actual activated criterion id.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Approach or Dependencies section: 'The criterion id `project.portability` is the final activated id confirmed by the criterion-integration child and may be used directly.' If it is not yet confirmed, replace hardcoded occurrences with a `<TBD: criterion-integration child id>` placeholder and add an explicit unblocking condition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the two overlay mutations needed to activate `project.portability` in the live coaching path: (1) add a `plan_review` routing entry for `project.portability` in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`, and (2) add `project.portability` to the overlay's `activate` list — or explicitly scope these to the criterion-integration child and add an AC proving that omitting them leaves the move inert (making the inertness intentional and testable).
evidence: Plan Approach: 'applies_when: [project.portability]'
Plan Dependencies: 'Depends on the criterion-integration child because the move trigger must use the final activated criterion id.'
`applicable_moves` in `coach.py:109`: 'return {mid: m for mid, m in registry.items() if move_applies(m, triggers)}' where triggers = {c for f in findings for c in f.get('criteria', [])}
`effective_criteria` in `overlay.py:276`: a project id is only active if it appears in the overlay `activate` list AND has a routing entry for the gate
ABSENCE: No mention of `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` edits (routing entry + activate) in Scope, Approach, or ACs
Cache finding at `.rebar/cache/plan-review/2ad8-f56e-7bc2-4948/59b353bc3aa1cc0b1572.json`: '`project.portability` ... does not yet exist in the codebase (criteria_routing.json)'
findings.finding: The `applies_when: [project.portability]` tag in the new move is a forward-reference to an unactivated criterion id. The `applicable_moves` function derives active triggers exclusively from `f.get('criteria', [])` on surviving findings; those criteria tags can only ever include ids that are (a) declared in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` under the `plan_review` gate key AND (b) listed in the overlay's `activate` array. The plan neither specifies these two overlay mutations nor references any file where they live, so the move will load and validate cleanly but remain permanently inert in the live path — `applicable_moves` will never match it. The plan simultaneously acknowledges the dependency on a 'criterion-integration child' to finalize the id, yet provides no specification of how `project.portability` enters the activated vocabulary.
scenarios: Live plan-review run: `project.portability` never appears in any finding's `criteria[]` because the criterion is not activated; `applicable_moves` returns {} for the new move; no coaching is ever emitted — the move is inert.
Test suite passes (ACs 5–7 inject synthetic trigger sets directly, bypassing the real activation path) while the live path never fires.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope and Approach: 'This ticket is move-definition only; the criterion-integration child activates `project.portability` in the overlay. Add AC: `applicable_moves(load_move_registry(repo_root), set())` returns `{}` for the new move (inert until criterion is activated).' This makes the planned inertness explicit and tested rather than an accidental structural gap.
